data.title: Iveco Group reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Iveco Group press release (OTCPK:IVCGF): Q1 adjusted diluted earnings per share of €0.31 (€0.57 in Q1 2024).
  • Consolidated revenues amounted to €3,026 million compared to €3,367 million in Q1 2024.

data.title: Mitsubishi UFJ Financial GAAP EPS of ¥159.48, revenue of ¥13630B; reaffims FY outlook
data.description:
• Mitsubishi UFJ Financial press release (NYSE:MUFG): Q1 GAAP EPS of ¥159.48.
  • Revenue of ¥13630B (+14.6% Y/Y).
  • Earnings Target for the Fiscal Year ending March 31, 2026 (Consolidated) MUFG has set an earnings target of 2,000.0 billion yen of profits attributable to owners of parent for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2026. MUFG is engaged in financial service businesses such as banking business, trust banking business, securities business and credit card/loan businesses. Because there are various uncertainties caused by economic situation, market environments and other factors in these businesses, MUFG discloses a target of its profits attributable to owners of parent instead of a forecast of its performance.

data.title: Walmart warns U.S. consumers may see higher prices even at the reduced tariff levels
data.description:
Walmart (NYSE:WMT) management warned during the retailer's earnings conference call and in media interviews that tariffs could lead to higher prices for U.S. consumers. 

"We’re wired for everyday low prices, but the magnitude of these increases is more than any retailer can absorb,"  highlighted Chief Financial Officer John David Rainey. "It’s more than any supplier can absorb. And so I’m concerned that consumer is going to start seeing higher prices. You’ll begin to see that, likely towards the tail end of this month, and then certainly much more in June," he added.

Walmart (NYSE:WMT) CEO Doug McMillon said the company will do its best to keep consumer prices as low as possible, but due to the magnitude of the tariffs, even at the reduced levels announced this week, the company can't absorb all the pressure given the reality of narrow retail margins. 

Walmart (NYSE:WMT) held off on issuing Q2 EPS and operating income guidance with its report.

During Q1, Walmart's (NYSE:WMT) comparable sales in the U.S. rose 4.5% to top the consensus estimate of 3.9%. Transactions were 1.6% higher during the quarter, and the average ticket was 2.8% higher compared to a year ago. E-commerce sales rose 21% during the quarter and contributed 350 basis points to comparable sales.

Shares of Walmart (WMT) were up 1.5% in premarket trading on Thursday.

data.title: US nears deal to allow UAE import millions of Nvidia's AI chips - report
data.description:
The U.S. has a preliminary agreement with the United Arab Emirates to allow it to import 500,000 of Nvidia's (NASDAQ:NVDA) advanced AI chips per year, beginning this year, Reuters reported citing people with knowledge of the matter.

The potential agreement was at least through 2027, but there is a chance that it could stretch to 2030, the report added.

The draft deal called for 20% of the chips, or 100,000 per year, to go to UAE's AI startup G42 while the rest would be split among U.S. companies with large AI operations such as Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT)  and Oracle (NYSE:ORCL), that may also look to build data centers in the UAE, the report noted.

Nvidia had already declined to comment on the matter when it was previously reported on Tuesday.

Microsoft, Oracle, the White House and G42 did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

The agreement is still being discussed and could change before being finalized. One person told the news outlet that the deal, elements of which were previously reported by the New York Times, was facing growing opposition in the U.S. government over the past day.

The preliminary deal also intends to promote data centers in the U.S. The agreement currently says that for every facility G42 builds in the UAE, it must build a similar one in the U.S., the report noted.

The definition of what is an advanced AI chip would be figured out in a separate working group which will be formed later, along with security requirements, the report added.

The proposed chips are for the most advanced graphics processing units, or GPUs, the report noted. Currently, it could refer to Nvidia Blackwell chips, which are more powerful than the previous generation of Hopper chips, or it could be Nvidia's upcoming Rubin chips, which are more powerful than both of these, according to the report.

On Tuesday, Bloomberg News reported that over the lifetime of the agreement, G42 can buy computing capabilities equal to about 1 million to 1.5 million Nvidia's H100 chips. This number is about four times more than G42 would have been allowed to purchase under the Biden-era chip export rule known as AI diffusion, which Trump plans to end.

U.S. President Donald Trump is on a tour of the Middle East this week and on Tuesday announced $600B worth of commitments from Saudi Arabia, including deals to purchase large quantities of chips from Nvidia, Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) and Qualcomm (QCOM).

data.title: Dollar Tree becomes just the third retailer with more than 9,000 stores in the U.S.
data.description:
Dollar Tree (NASDAQ:DLTR) announced on Thursday that it surpassed a major retail milestone with the opening of its 9,000th store. The company said the development reinforces its position as one of the fastest-growing and most powerful value retailers in North America. 

"Reaching 9,000 stores is a powerful indicator of the strength of our brand and the loyalty of our associates and customers," highlighted Chief Executive Officer Mike Creedon. "It’s also a testament to our profitable growth strategy, creating long-term value for our shareholders," he added.

The retailer plans to commemorate the achievement locally with a grand opening celebration at the official 9,000th store in Plano, Texas. 

The only other retailers with more than 9,000 stores in the U.S. are Walmart (WMT) and CVS Health (CVS). Walgreen Boots Alliance (WBA) has more than 8,000 stores.

Shares of Dollar Tree (NASDAQ:DLTR) edged 0.2% higher in premarket trading on Thursday, amid a gentle rally in the retail sector following Walmart's (WMT) earnings report.

data.title: Biohaven slides as FDA extends review period for neuro drug
data.description:
Shares of Biohaven (NYSE:BHVN) fell ~11% in the premarket on Thursday after the company said that the FDA has extended the review period of its marketing application for its lead drug troriluzole, targeted at spinocerebellar ataxia (SCA), a group of rare neurodegenerative disorders.

Previously, the new drug application for troriluzole was under the FDA’s priority review with a target action date expected in Q3 2025. With the FDA's Office of Neuroscience delaying the review by three months, the company now expects a potential approval of troriluzole in Q4 2025.

Biohaven (NYSE:BHVN) said that, according to a letter from the Division of Neurology 1 within FDA's Office of Neuroscience, the delay was due to a requirement to fully review new submissions made by the company in response to the agency’s requests.

The division has also indicated its plans to hold an advisory committee meeting to discuss the NDA. No date has yet been fixed for the AdCom meeting.

BHVN, a spinout of Biohaven Pharmaceutical when Pfizer (PFE) acquired the migraine therapy maker in 2022, noted that the FDA did not raise any additional concerns in the letter.

data.title: WELL Health Technologies looks to conduct normal course issuer bid
data.description:
• WELL Health Technologies (TSX:WELL:CA) (OTC:WHTCF) said the notice of intention to make a normal course issuer bid the company had filed with the Toronto Stock Exchange has been approved.
  • Under the bid, the company can acquire up to an aggregate of 6.33M shares over the next 12-month period.

data.title: JPMorgan Chase credit card net charge-off rate drops in April
data.description:
JPMorgan Chase's (NYSE:JPM) credit card master trust's delinquency rate stayed among the lowest in the industry, while its net charge-off rate fell significantly, according to data the bank released on Thursday. Both remain below prepandemic levels.

The Chase Issuance Trust's delinquency rate of 0.89% in April was unchanged from March and rose from 0.81% in April 2024. It remains well below its April 2019 level of 1.18%.

The trust's net charge-off rate of 1.67% dropped from 1.85% in March and compared with 1.48% a year ago. That's also still better than the 2.51% rate in April 2019.

Principal receivables at month-end were $12.4B, up 1.6% M/M and down 3.1% Y/Y.
